name: MCP Tools Implementation (server/tools/)
applicability: Production MCP server with OAuth scope filtering, Zod validation, tracing, and presenter-based responses. 7 tool modules covering documents, collections, comments, attachments, users, fetch, utilities.
home_connection: JASON-OS Domain 02a (MCP infrastructure). SoNash has 3 MCP servers configured but none are first-party production MCP servers. Outline's approach of bridging MCP context to existing API commands via buildAPIContext() is directly applicable.

name: Command Pattern (server/commands/)
applicability: Multi-model business logic encapsulated in command files (~8700 lines). Both API routes and MCP tools delegate to same commands. Clean separation avoids logic duplication across surfaces.
home_connection: SoNash uses Cloud Functions (httpsCallable) for mutations. The command pattern could abstract business logic from the Cloud Function transport, enabling future MCP tool surface without duplicating logic.

name: Plugin System (PluginManager + Hook enum)
applicability: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.). Priority-based ordering. Auto-discovery via glob. plugin.json manifests. Client/server/shared split per plugin.
home_connection: SoNash skills are stored in .claude/skills/ but have no runtime plugin system. The PluginManager pattern (hook types, priority ordering, auto-discovery) could inform JASON-OS extensibility architecture.

name: Presenter Pattern (server/presenters/)
applicability: JSON response formatting layer between backend models and frontend/MCP consumers. Each model has a presenter that controls what fields are exposed and how they're formatted.
home_connection: SoNash uses Firestore directly from Cloud Functions. A presenter layer would help standardize API responses if SoNash ever adds an API surface.

name: ARCHITECTURE.md
applicability: Clear directory tree documentation for monorepo. 68 lines covering frontend, backend, and shared structure with per-directory annotations.
home_connection: SoNash lacks an equivalent architecture document. The format (directory tree + annotations) is simple and effective.

name: SERVICES.md
applicability: Documents 5 backend services (Admin, Web, Websockets, Worker, Collaboration) and how to run them independently via --services flag.
home_connection: SoNash is a single-service Next.js app. Service decomposition not applicable currently.

name: TRANSLATION.md
applicability: CrowdIn-based i18n workflow with automated PR sync. Step-by-step with screenshots.
home_connection: SoNash has no i18n currently. Low immediate relevance but the workflow documentation quality is a good template.

name: CLAUDE.md (Outline's version)
applicability: 197-line CLAUDE.md covering stack conventions, class ordering, TypeScript rules, React/MobX patterns, security guidance. Includes ProseMirror-specific sanitization rules and ORM migration patterns.
home_connection: Direct comparison with SoNash's 135-line CLAUDE.md. Outline's is more prescriptive on class organization and code style but less focused on behavioral guardrails and agent/skill workflows. Complementary approaches.

name: AGENTS.md (1-line stub)
applicability: Single line pointing to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. Not a useful AI workflow document.
home_connection: SoNash has no AGENTS.md equivalent — agent definitions live in .claude/agents/. Outline's approach is minimal.

name: Onboarding Documents (server/onboarding/)
applicability: 4 markdown templates injected into new team workspaces: 'What is Outline', 'Getting Started', 'Our Editor', 'Integrations & API'. Shows content-as-code pattern for user onboarding.
home_connection: SoNash has no onboarding content system. The pattern of storing user-facing onboarding as markdown templates could inform M7.3 Nashville & Knowledge milestone.

name: Queue/Event Processor System
applicability: Bull-based job queue with ~20 specialized processors (email, notifications, file operations, imports, integrations). BaseProcessor pattern. HealthMonitor for queue health.
home_connection: SoNash uses Cloud Functions for async work. Bull queue pattern relevant if SoNash moves to a more complex backend architecture.

name: Model Decorator System
applicability: Custom Sequelize decorators: @Changeset (audit trail), @CounterCache (denormalized counts), @Deprecated (migration helper), @Encrypted (field-level encryption), @Fix (data migration).
home_connection: SoNash uses Firestore with no ORM. The @Encrypted decorator pattern is relevant for M4.5 Security & Privacy milestone — field-level encryption at the model layer.

name: Vendored rfc6902 with Performance Fix
applicability: Vendorized JSON Patch library with documented upstream performance fix PR. Clean vendor practice with README explaining why.
home_connection: Good vendor documentation pattern. SoNash has no vendored dependencies currently.
